originalTitle: 'Be serious... don't speak every day': Macron criticises Trump approach to Iran war
title: Big Leaders, Big Talks: Thinking Before We Speak!
content:
Hello, my wonderful friends! Your news friend from sunny Africa is here today with some very important news. It's about big leaders from big countries, and how they talk to each other. It's like when you and your friends talk, but on a much, much bigger scale! Sometimes, leaders need to be super careful with their words, just like you learn to be kind with your words at school. Let's find out why!

Meet Our Leaders!

Imagine you have a head teacher at your school, right? Well, countries also have very important people who are like their head teachers! They are called Presidents or Prime Ministers. They help make important decisions for everyone in their country. Today, we are talking about two such leaders. One is Mr. Macron. He is the President of a beautiful country far away called France. France is in a continent called Europe. It's known for its yummy pastries and a very tall tower called the Eiffel Tower! Mr. Macron works very hard to help the people of France. The other leader we are talking about is Mr. Trump. He used to be the President of a very big country called the United States of America. We often call it America for short. America is also very far away, across a big ocean! Mr. Trump was in charge of America for some time. Did you know? The world has nearly 200 different countries, and each one has its own leaders who try to make things good for their people!

When Leaders Talk Big

Sometimes, when people talk a lot, very often, or very quickly, they might say things that are not very clear. Or they might change their mind after saying something. It can be a little confusing, right? Mr. Macron thought that Mr. Trump was talking too much, perhaps even every day, about some very serious things. He felt that it's super important for leaders to think very, very carefully before they speak. Why is that? Well, when a President speaks, everyone in their country, and even people in other countries, listen very closely! Their words are very powerful. They can make people happy, or worried, or even confused. So, they need to choose their words like choosing the right puzzle piece – very carefully! It's a bit like when your teacher asks you a question. You think about the answer before you say it out loud, so you can give the best, clearest answer. Leaders have to do that too, but for really, really big problems that affect millions of people.

What is "Being Serious" Important?

Mr. Macron said leaders need to "be serious." What does that mean? It means they should take their job very, very seriously. They should think deeply, be responsible, and understand all the different parts of a problem. When leaders talk about important things like peace, or other countries, or big plans, they need to be serious. They can't just joke around or say the first thing that comes to their mind. They need to show that they understand how important these things are for everyone. Imagine you are building a tall tower with blocks. You need to be serious and careful to make sure it doesn't fall down, right? Leaders are like that, building a stable and safe future for their countries with their words and actions. Did you know? Many leaders meet together in special places to talk about world problems and try to find peaceful solutions!

What Does "Contradictory" Mean?

Here is a big, long word: "contradictory"! It just means saying different things at different times about the same thing. For example, imagine you tell your friend, "Yes, let's play football!" and then an hour later you say, "No, I don't want to play football, I want to play netball!" Your friend might be very confused, right? They wouldn't know what you really want to do! Well, sometimes Mr. Trump's words were a bit like that. He would say one thing, and then later say something that was a little different. This can make other countries and leaders confused too. It's much better when leaders say things clearly and stick to what they say. It helps everyone understand and trust each other. Trust is very important when countries need to work together.

What About Countries Like Iran?

The leaders, Mr. Macron and Mr. Trump, were talking about a country called Iran. Iran is another country, also far away from us, in a part of the world called the Middle East. Sometimes, countries can have big disagreements. These disagreements can be about many things, like rules, or land, or what they believe is right. When disagreements get very, very big, sometimes people worry about something very sad called a "war." A war is when countries fight each other with soldiers and weapons. It is a terrible thing because people get hurt, homes are broken, and children can become very scared. Nobody wants war. Because war is so bad, leaders try very hard to talk and solve problems without fighting. That is why Mr. Macron was saying that leaders must be serious and clear when they talk about these very important, sensitive situations. Did you know? Many children around the world dream of a future where there is only peace and no wars!

Why Peace is the Best Way!

Just like in your classroom, when you have a problem with a friend, what's the best thing to do? You talk about it, right? You try to understand each other and find a way to be friends again. It's the same for countries! Leaders must talk, talk, talk! They must listen to each other's ideas and try to find a solution that is fair for everyone. This way of talking and working together is called "diplomacy," and it's much better than fighting. When leaders talk carefully and clearly, it helps prevent misunderstandings and stops small problems from growing into big, scary ones. It helps keep the world a safer and happier place for all of us, especially for children like you!

Let's All Talk Nicely!

So, the big message from Mr. Macron is that leaders should think very carefully, be serious, and be clear with their words. This helps everyone understand, trust each other, and work together for peace. It shows us that even big leaders need to remember the power of their words. And it reminds us all that talking nicely, listening carefully, and being clear are super important skills for everyone, everywhere! Let's all try to do that every day. Did you know? When you talk and listen carefully to your friends, you are practicing being a good leader too, because you are building understanding and friendship!
summary: A leader from France, Mr. Macron, said that leaders should be very serious and careful with their words, and not talk too much or change their minds often, especially when discussing big problems like disagreements between countries, because clear talking helps keep the world peaceful.
whyMatters: This matters because when big leaders talk carefully and clearly, it helps countries understand each other better and work for peace instead of fighting, keeping children and everyone else safe and happy.

title: Iran's two largest steel plants shut down due to strikes, companies say
content: The strikes, which Iran says were first launched by Israel in co-ordination with the US last week, could cause major damage to the Iranian economy.

originalTitle: Brazil grants joint custody over pets in new law for separated couples
title: Super Cool News for Pets in Brazil!
content:
Jambo, Jambo! Hello, Awesome Kids!

Welcome to your special news report from me, your friendly reporter from Africa! Today, we have some really exciting news all the way from a big, colourful country called Brazil. It’s a place far, far away, across the ocean. This news is super important for anyone who loves their pets – and I know that's all of you!

Pets Are Family, Right?

Do you have a dog, a cat, a fish, or maybe a funny parrot at home? If you do, you know how special they are! Pets are not just animals; they are part of our families. They play with us, they cuddle with us, and they make us laugh. When you feel sad, your pet might even come and sit by you to make you feel better. They listen to all our secrets without telling anyone! That’s why we love them so much, and that’s why they deserve the best care, always.

Big New Rules in Brazil for Pets!

So, what's the big news from Brazil? Well, sometimes, Mummy and Daddy decide to live in different houses. This can be a bit tricky, right? But now, in Brazil, there's a new rule especially for pets when this happens! The people who make the rules in Brazil have said that pets should get to spend time with both Mummy and Daddy, even if they live apart. It's like a special sharing rule for our furry, feathery, or scaly friends!

What Does 'Joint Custody' Mean?

That big word, 'joint custody,' just means sharing! Imagine you have a favourite toy, and you want to share it with your best friend. You might play with it for a while, and then your friend plays with it for a while. That's a bit like joint custody for pets. It means that the pet will spend some time living with Mummy and then some time living with Daddy. This way, the pet gets love and care from both homes. It's a fair way to make sure the pet doesn't miss anyone too much!

Why Did Brazil Make This Rule?

Brazil made this new rule because the people who make the rules, called lawmakers, know how important pets are. They understand that pets are not just things, like a table or a chair. Pets have feelings! They get happy, they get excited, and they can also get sad or confused if their routine changes too much. So, the lawmakers thought, "If children get to spend time with both Mummy and Daddy, why shouldn't our beloved pets?" They want to make sure pets stay happy and loved, no matter what changes happen in the family.

Did You Know? Fun Pet Facts!

* Did you know that dogs can learn hundreds of words? Some clever dogs can even understand up to 1,000 words! * Did you know that cats purr not just when they are happy, but sometimes when they are scared or healing? * Did you know that hamsters carry food in their cheeks, which can stretch out like big pouches? * Did you know that fish can recognize the people who feed them?

Brazil: A Land Far Away!

Brazil is a very big and beautiful country in South America. It's famous for its colourful carnivals, its amazing football players, and its huge rainforest with lots of different animals. People in Brazil love music, dancing, and having fun. And it seems they also love their pets very much, which is why they made this special new rule! They speak a language called Portuguese there. Imagine learning a new language just to say "Hello" to a Brazilian dog!

How Does This Help Pets?

This new rule is fantastic for pets! It means they won't feel lonely or confused if their human family moves to different houses. They will still get their yummy food, their fun walks, and all the cuddles from both their Mummy and Daddy. It helps the pet feel secure and loved, knowing they are important to everyone. It also helps the grown-ups decide easily how to care for their pet together, without any arguments.

More "Did You Know?" About Pets!

* Did you know that a group of cats is called a clowder? * Did you know that some parrots can live for more than 80 years, even longer than humans? * Did you know that rabbits like to 'binky' when they are happy? It means they jump and twist in the air! * Did you know that having a pet can help you learn to be responsible and kind?

A Happy Future for Pets!

This new rule in Brazil shows us all how much pets truly mean to us. It's a wonderful step to make sure our animal friends are always happy and cared for, no matter what. It sets a great example for other countries around the world to think about how important pets are in our lives. So, let's all give a big cheer for the pets in Brazil!

That's All for Today!

Remember to always be kind to animals and give your pets lots of love. They give us so much joy, and they deserve all our care in return. Until next time, my little news explorers, stay curious and keep loving those amazing animals! Kwaheri!
summary: Brazil has made a new rule that lets pets live with both Mummy and Daddy when they live in different houses, just like kids do.
whyMatters: This new rule is important because it makes sure pets are always happy and loved by both their owners, showing how much pets are like family.
